content: For a single-process real-time game/sim loop, a single-threaded loop with a fixed-timestep accumulator is the standard and simplest correct approach -- no threading or asyncio needed for the core sim+render loop. Python's GIL means threading gives no CPU-bound speedup and adds complexity/race-condition risk for shared aircraft state between a sim thread and render thread. asyncio is useful only if the app also does non-blocking I/O (e.g. background file loads, future network play) concurrently with the loop -- it doesn't help pure CPU-bound per-tick simulation math. Recommendation: single-threaded main loop with the accumulator pattern; do not introduce asyncio or threading for the sim/render split.
